description:
You are a security auditor who must identify every **world-writable regular file** inside the existing project tree
/home/user/project.

1. Scan the directory  /home/user/project  (recursively) for files that meet **all** of the following criteria  
   • type: regular file (not a directory, symlink, socket, etc.)  
   • the file has write permission for “others” (world-writable)  

2. Create the directory  /home/user/audit  if it does not already exist.  
3. Write the findings to the log file  /home/user/audit/world_writable_list.log.

Exact log-file format (the automated checker is case- and space-sensitive):

• One matching file per line.  
• Lines must be sorted in **lexicographical (byte) order**.  
• Each line must contain two fields separated by a single pipe (“|”) character:  
    PATH|PERMS  
  where  
    PATH  = absolute path to the file (e.g. /home/user/project/data.db)  
    PERMS = the 10-character permission string exactly as produced by “ls -l” for that file  
            (e.g. -rw-rw-rw-)  

Example line (do **not** include this in your output):  
/abs/path/example.txt|-rw-rw-rw-

Your deliverable is only the correctly formatted log file.  No extra text, headers or trailing blank lines are allowed in /home/user/audit/world_writable_list.log.
truth:
Initial on-disk layout before the agent starts:

/home/user/project/  
├── data.db                 (-rw-rw-rw-)       user user  
├── file1.sh                (-rw-r--r--)       user user  
├── file2.txt               (-rw-rw-r--)       user user  
├── logs                    (drwxrwxrwx)       user user  
│   └── app.log             (-rw-r--r--)       user user  
├── scripts                 (drwxr-xr-x)       user user  
│   └── install.sh          (-rwxr-xr-x)       user user  
└── tmp                     (drwxrwxrwt)       user user  
    └── cache.tmp           (-rw-rw-rw-)       user user  

There are exactly two **regular files** that are world-writable:

1. /home/user/project/data.db         permissions: -rw-rw-rw-  
2. /home/user/project/tmp/cache.tmp   permissions: -rw-rw-rw-

Required final state for automated verification:

A file called
   /home/user/audit/world_writable_list.log
must exist and contain **exactly** the following two lines (including the terminating newline on the second line, no extra blank lines):

/home/user/project/data.db|-rw-rw-rw-
/home/user/project/tmp/cache.tmp|-rw-rw-rw-

The file must be world-readable but not world-writable (mode 0644 or stricter).  
The directory /home/user/audit/ must exist with default user-writable permissions (0755 or stricter).  
No other changes to the file system are expected.
